detail: The spec-judge proved a pairwise contradiction between two scenarios this CR added. 'cyber-mux issues every split the tree names and never pre-flights the region' demanded every split be issued given a pool that cannot fit, while 'a split the backend refuses names the pane it could not create' demanded the walk stop at the refusal. Both Givens are satisfied by one constructible snapshot and neither subsumes the other, so there was no intended winner. The first scenario was simply false of the correct implementation on tmux — it held only on a backend that refuses nothing. Repaired by rewriting it as 'a backend that refuses no split builds the whole pool, however small the panes', which dissolves the conflict by making the Givens disjoint and homes the herdr half of the stated portability cost, which no scenario had covered.

The spec-judge checked the explore measurement's arithmetic and found the durable spec asserted a tmux floor of roughly 3 rows by 3 columns per pane that its own table refuted at two of four datapoints. Root cause was the spike, not the prose: the first pass used a powers-of-two ladder, so every reported ceiling was a lower bound rather than a ceiling, which produced the artifact of two different region widths both reporting the same 32 even-horizontal and invited a tidy generalization. Re-measured by binary search with linear verification around each boundary; feasibility proved monotone at every boundary. The real ceilings are materially different and cells-per-pane at the ceiling is not constant, drifting across the measured range, so no per-pane floor exists to model. The claim was replaced with the measurement rather than softened, and the finding strengthens the CR's own decision not to pre-flight the region.

The spec-judge found this CR falsifies a factual claim in a sibling node's prose: the mux node stated the Exec seam discards a backend's stderr and that a follow-up owns the gap. This CR is partially that follow-up — the seam now captures stderr into an optional lastError. Corrected the mux node's prose to say the gap narrows rather than closes: send keys still does not read lastError and a null still cannot be told from an empty stdout. The scenarios stay in the layout suite per the house style this node already set for seam additions driven by layout, which issue #10 tracks.

Round 1's scenario contradiction was moved rather than fixed. The round-1 repair cleared the first pair, but the two reason-reporting scenarios then crossed: one Given spoke the BACKEND giving its reason, the other spoke the RUNNER reporting none. Orthogonal actors, so the sets cross rather than nest, and the intersecting snapshot — backend emits the reason on stderr, runner drops it — is the exact pre-CR state this CR's own design doc describes, and remains live against a real tmux. Repaired by making both Givens speak the runner, the one axis that actually decides the Then: lastError set versus null.

A Then asserting cyber-mux refuses nothing of its own accord was a claim about decision provenance rather than an observable, and was unloseable: no subject could pass the scenario's other two steps and fail it. Dropped. The scenario still catches the pre-flighting implementation the user rejected, via every pane the template names is created and it exits 0.

The re-measured numbers were correct and were then over-read, the same defect class as round 1 in a new form. The claim that no constant per-pane floor exists was evidenced by a drifting average cells-per-pane, but a constant floor plus uneven panes predicts exactly that drift, so the evidence was vacuous — and the companion causal claim, that the ceiling emerges from the comb's cumulative ratio rounding, was refuted by the CR's own table: tiled is not a comb yet its ceiling factors into a grid whose row count equals even-vertical's ceiling at all three regions where both were measured. Retreated to the only claim the data supports, that no rule cyber-mux could rely on predicts the ceiling, which is sufficient to settle the rejection of pre-flighting. The recurring lesson across both rounds is recorded in the design doc: the defect was never the measurement, always the explanation.

The measurement table, the herdr probe and the portability dent were each carried verbatim in both the node README and the design doc, while the spec itself called the numbers the backend's to change. Hand-maintenance drift had already begun. De-duplicated: the design doc section that IS this question owns the numbers, the README cites it and keeps only the conclusion. Also reconciled the two homes' disclaimers, which had barred asserted numbers for different reasons at different scopes — the design doc's determinism argument read as licensing a pinned-tmux assertion the README barred categorically.

After three spec-gate rounds that failed Builder and Architect on the prose rather than the data, the user cut scope to the doc and the lastError change, with no suite edit and no new tests. The four added scenarios and the layout node README were reverted to HEAD, so the node's spec and its frozen suite are untouched. The CR therefore carries no spec-behavior change and no spec gate applies: no .feature diff (verified 0 added / 0 modified / 0 removed against HEAD), no status transition, no freeze interaction. The mux node's prose correction stands because this CR falsifies it — unfrozen prose, a factual fix, not a behavior claim. The measurement that answers the issue lands in the design doc, which is the natural home since this CR is that doc's own Q4. The scope cut also removes the surface all three rounds kept failing on rather than attempting a fourth explanation.

Drove the change end to end against live tmux 3.6b on a throwaway -L server pinned to 80x8, rather than trusting the unit suite. A too-small split now reports tmux split-window failed followed by the backend's own no space for new pane, where before it reported only the bare failure. Also confirmed the staleness hazard the round-3 judge identified is closed: after a subsequent SUCCESSFUL command the runner's recorded reason clears to undefined, so withReason falls back to the bare message rather than attributing an unrelated command's reason to a later failure. That hazard is real rather than theoretical because the exists probe and the mux probe run commands that fail routinely. Typecheck passes and the full unit suite is green at 415 tests, unchanged, which is the point of the callable-interface shape: no existing fake sets lastError, so no existing message moved.

The user reversed the no-tests scope decision and asked for the lastError tests, so the backlog follow-up recording that the clear-on-success guard was untested is absorbed into this CR rather than left outstanding, and its ledger line is removed because it no longer describes any outstanding work. The herdr no-floor follow-up stands, since it is upstream's to fix. Added exec.test.ts covering realExec's stdout/null contract, stderr capture, the clear-on-success guard, the failed-silently and missing-binary cases that must record no reason rather than an empty one, and withReason's two branches; plus one throw-path test per adapter proving the reason reaches the caller and that a runner setting nothing still yields the bare message. Every new test was mutation-checked rather than trusted for being green: dropping the clear-on-success fails the guard, forcing stderr back to ignore fails two, and dropping withReason from the tmux throw site fails the adapter test.
